---
title: "Program Adoption: Waffle Chart"
author: "Ibraheem Saqib Ellahi"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: 
  html_document:
    theme: flatly
    toc: false
    code_folding: hide
params:
  # Master Data Path
  data_path: "../1_data/master_micro_survey.csv"
  
  # Variables
  status_var: "tech_adopted"
  
  # Policy-Facing Labels
  success_label: "Successfully Adopted"
  failure_label: "Pending / Opted Out"
  metric_name: "Hardware Rollout Adoption"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = TRUE,
  warning = FALSE,
  message = FALSE,
  fig.path = "../4_output/figures/",
  fig.width = 7,
  fig.height = 5, 
  fig.retina = 3
)
```

```{r load-and-prep}
library(tidyverse)

# Load data
raw_data <- read_csv(params$data_path)

# ==============================================================================
# DATA SCRUBBING & PROCESSING BLOCK
# ==============================================================================
# 1. Calculate the exact percentage of success from raw microdata
summary_stats <- raw_data %>%
  # Safely drop NAs only for the required status variable
  drop_na(!!sym(params$status_var)) %>%
  
  # Scrub text inputs and map messy data to a binary success outcome
  mutate(
    Clean_Status = str_to_title(str_trim(as.character(!!sym(params$status_var)))),
    Is_Success = str_detect(Clean_Status, "(?i)Yes|1|True|Adopt|Success")
  ) %>%
  
  # Aggregate up to the final percentages
  summarize(
    total = n(),
    successes = sum(Is_Success, na.rm = TRUE),
    pct = round((successes / total) * 100),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# 2. Build the 10x10 Waffle Grid mathematically
waffle_data <- expand.grid(x = 1:10, y = 1:10) %>%
  arrange(y, x) %>% # Fill the grid bottom-to-top, left-to-right
  mutate(
    id = row_number(),
    
    # Assign labels using the YAML parameters, wrapping text for clean legends
    Category = ifelse(id <= summary_stats$pct, 
                      str_wrap(params$success_label, width = 20), 
                      str_wrap(params$failure_label, width = 20)),
                      
    # Lock factor levels to maintain strict color consistency
    Category = factor(Category, levels = c(
      str_wrap(params$success_label, width = 20), 
      str_wrap(params$failure_label, width = 20)
    ))
  )
```

## Program Adoption Overview

```{r plot-waffle}
source("../2_R/2.0_setup_theme.R", local = TRUE)

# Dynamically assign colors to the wrapped categories
waffle_colors <- setNames(
  c(unname(cerp_cols["primary"]), unname(cerp_cols["neutral"])), 
  c(str_wrap(params$success_label, width = 20), str_wrap(params$failure_label, width = 20))
)

ggplot(waffle_data, aes(x = x, y = y, fill = Category)) +
  
  # geom_tile creates the squares. The thick white border creates the "waffle" separation.
  geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 2) +
  
  # coord_equal strictly locks the aspect ratio so squares don't stretch into rectangles
  coord_equal() +
  
  scale_fill_manual(values = waffle_colors) +
  
  theme_cerp() +
  
  # Strip all axes, text, and gridlines to leave a completely clean grid
  theme(
    axis.text = element_blank(),
    axis.title = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks = element_blank(),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "top",
    legend.title = element_blank(),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 11, face = "bold")
  ) +
  
  labs(
    title = paste("Adoption Rate:", params$metric_name),
    subtitle = paste0("Each square represents 1% of the total sample. Current rate: ", summary_stats$pct, "%."),
    caption = "Generated via CERP Analytics Template"
  )
```
